protocol: blind double annotation (2 independent auditors per row, labels hidden) + tie-break adjudication against the five-way contract

conventions_pinned.C1_contradiction_precedence: If any subclaim of a composite claim is contradicted by the span, the label is contradicts, even if other subclaims are supported. partial_support is reserved for supported-plus-ABSENT, never supported-plus-conflicted.
conventions_pinned.C2_period_binding: When a claim binds figures to a reporting period the span does not state: if the source document reports a single period (earnings release headline figures), source identity satisfies the binding and verified_support is allowed at reduced confidence; if the document contains multiple periods (e.g., 10-Q quarterly and nine-month sections), the binding is unverifiable from the span and the row is capped at partial_support (other elements supported) or insufficient (binding is the substantive assertion).
conventions_pinned.C3_materially_weakens: A span implying a lower point/milestone estimate than the claim's figure materially weakens the claim under span-only judgment and is labeled contradicts, with staleness recorded in the note when the conflict stems from forecast vintage.

decisions.rationale: Span states non-GAAP gross margin expected at approximately 56%; the claim asserts 58%. Under C1 a conflicted subclaim forces contradicts. Original seed label treated a conflicting figure as a missing one.
Figures match exactly; the FY26 Q3 binding is satisfied by single-period source identity under C2 (press release headline figures).

The 10-Q contains near-identical quarterly ($12.8B) and nine-month ($36.5B) paragraphs and the span does not state the period; under C2 the quarterly binding is unverifiable from the span, so the row is capped at partial_support. Must use the same ruler as its trap counterpart 0020 (insufficient).
One auditor omitted this row from output (31/32 lines); the other confirmed. Span plainly states both claim elements.

Span quotes SIA's CEO framing $1 trillion as the 2026 trajectory as of May 4; under C3 this materially weakens the $1.5 trillion claim in span-only judgment, consistent with the Deloitte $975B row. The forecast-vintage (staleness) lesson is preserved in this note.
